name: YouTube Transcript
one_liner: TranscriptAPI (transcriptapi.com) is a hosted REST API for extracting YouTube video transcripts plus searching videos/channels and browsing channels and playlists.
access_notes: Sign up gives 100 free credits with no card required, yielding a working API key immediately. Paid plans ($5/mo for 1,000 credits) extend usage, but the free credits are enough to make real API calls without payment or approval.
api_notes: REST API (v2) over HTTPS GET requests returning JSON, Bearer-token auth. ~10 endpoints: transcript, video info, search, channel resolve/search/videos/latest, and playlist videos. Rate limited at 300 req/min; credit-based (1 credit per successful request, channel-latest is free).
mcp_notes: Vendor ships an official hosted MCP server at https://transcriptapi.com/mcp, documented at transcriptapi.com/docs/mcp/ with an MCP dashboard. Supports OAuth (Claude/ChatGPT) and API key (OpenAI Agent Builder) auth.
main_blocker: None significant; free tier is capped at 100 credits so sustained use requires a low-cost paid plan, but building/testing a tool is fully self-serve.

evidence.claim: Bearer-token auth with API key from dashboard; example curl to /api/v2/youtube/search
100 free credits to try the API, no card required; $5/mo for 1,000 credits
Official vendor MCP server at https://transcriptapi.com/mcp with OAuth and API key auth

open_questions: Whether the id-99 'YouTube Transcript' app refers specifically to transcriptapi.com vs alternatives (e.g., youtube-transcript.io or the open-source jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api Python library); the hint points to transcriptapi.com, which this record covers.
